jd_text: The role of the Schools Programmes Unit Head has responsibility for the establishment and management of relationships with k-12 educational institutions in Abu Dhabi and the UAE, and the introduction of outreach programs designed to enhance an understanding and curriculum in the arts. Academic institutions, community members, officials, and other non-profit organizations by developing the museums university & community engagement strategy and priorities, establishing partnerships, creating university and community engagement opportunities, managing and coordinating all activities linked to engaging these defined audience segments with educational and cultural activities and programs. Oversees the development and implementation of engagement strategies and plans of the museum for the development of partnerships and relationships with academic institutions and community groups to build awareness and new audiences for the museum. • Prepares and manages the budget for all unit initiatives and programmes. • Manages the staff of the unit including recruitment & selection, performance management, mentoring & coaching, career development guidance, setting objectives and KPI’s of their direct reports. Evaluate educational programs and generate reports to discuss future requirements with the Education Manager and Senior Public Programs Manager to ensure continuous improvement and success of initiatives. • Supervision of Education Coordinators and volunteers in the delivery of educational programs and services to visitors and students. Pre-Opening • Support the Education PProgrammes Section Head & the Departmental Director in the preparation of the short and medium term plans for the unit in the run up the opening, opening events and first year of operations and assist in establishing priorities, methods, policies and procedures for implementing programmes in alignment with the strategic vision, mission and goals. • Oversee the recruitment and selection of all staff of the Unit. • Identify and develop partnerships and relationship with ADEK and k-12 educational companies and schools. Outgoing • Develop, implement and evaluate engaging outreach programmes designed around museum careers for P – 12 students Work with the Interpretation team to ensure the preparation of all support materials including student and family guides, educator guides and resource materials for the programmes. • Liaising with ADEC / schools organizing tailor-made workshops for the students. • Working with the Interpretation, publications and marketing teams in the preparation of promotional copy and publication materials for the educational programs. • Assisting in the training of Visitor Services Coordinators enabling the provision of educational tours of exhibitions. • Implement a strategy for continued relationship opportunities with educational institutions, consisting of regular updates, meetings, and special events sessions for educators. • Oversees the preparation of progress and status reports by analysing and summarizing the information, trends and forecasts of visits. • Contribute to the development of departmental plans which project funding, feasibility, staffing, and related constraints of the department ensuring the availability of required resources to carry out the strategy and initiatives. • Research the provision of K-12 education programming at other museums, heritage sites and visitor attractions locally, nationally and internationally. • Supports the development and coordination of all programmes including research, collection, live, publishing, education and interpretive programs, as required. • Cultivates and maintains effective relationships with K-12 educators and students throughout the UAE. Minimum of 5 years of experience with at least • 3 or more years experience in delivering an educational program for a museum or cultural institution or educational organization; • proven ability to design and implement educational programs, • proven ability to lead / manage teams. • Project management and planning skills. • Experience budgeting and tracking spending. • Experience in similar position in educational learning • Knowledge of current practice in audience development and museum education • Fluency in Arabic and Englis
